mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent economic contraction, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative 60-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days exclusively during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month change exceeds 0.4%, or signal direction reverses in high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment gains reflect sustained economic expansion and rising corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS increases are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in >65% of observations, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 255.0 indicates entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates robust aggregate demand, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 20-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing output and reduced corporate earnings capacity, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days specifically during economic contraction phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in >65% of contraction periods, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE falls below 3.5%.
